title: ilex ambigua subsp. montana
intro: Ilex ambigua subsp. montana, also known as the mountain holly, is a subspecies of the ilex ambigua plant that is native to the mountainous regions of certain parts of Europe. It is a type of evergreen shrub or small tree that has been used in traditional medicine for centuries due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of mountain holly include reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and improving digestion, as well as its potential to protect against certain diseases such as cancer and cardiovascular disease. In herbal preparations, mountain holly can be used as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties and health benefits.
